title: Tapping for Turning Sixty-Plus
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the identity shift that arrives with this decade
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the grief and disorientation of entering a decade that arrives with its own weight. The script addresses the identity gap between who you have been and who this chapter is asking you to become.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the arrival of a new decade brings disorientation, grief about what has not happened, or uncertainty about what this chapter is for.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the weight of a new decade. The identity that carried you here does not always fit the one this chapter is asking for. The script works on the gap between them.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I am entering a decade I was not prepared for, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am not sure who I am in this chapter, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though this decade carries grief and freedom in the same breath, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** This decade is a different country.
**Side of eye:** I was not prepared for how it would feel.
**Under eye:** Some of what I thought I would have, I do not have.
**Under nose:** Some of what I thought I would be, I am not.
**Chin:** There is grief in that, and I am allowed it.
**Collarbone:** The identity that carried me here does not fit quite right any more.
**Under arm:** I am not sure what the next chapter looks like.
**Top of head:** This is not the end. It is something I have not been before.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I know things now that I could not have known earlier.
**Side of eye:** There is weight to my experience.
**Under eye:** The losses taught me what I value.
**Under nose:** I am not starting from scratch. I am starting from accumulated ground.
**Chin:** I get to decide what this chapter looks like.
**Collarbone:** There is freedom in not performing a self I have outgrown.
**Under arm:** I have time. More than I think.
**Top of head:** This chapter is mine to write.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
